concepts.title: Focal chord
concepts.conceptBody:
Terms Related to an Ellipse

Let the equation of the ellipse

\(\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1\)

Centre : All chords passing through $\text{C}$ are bisected at $\text{C}$

Here $\text{C}\equiv \left( 0,0 \right)$

  1. Foci : $S$ and ${S}'$ are two foci of the ellipse and their co-ordinates are (ae, 0$)$ and (- ae, 0$)$ respectively
  2. Directrices : $ZM$ and ${Z}'{M}'$ are two directrices of the ellipse and their equation are $x=\frac{a}{e}$ and $x=-\,\,\frac{a}{e}$ respectively.
  3. Axes : The lines $\text{A}{{\text{A}}^{\text{ }\!\!'\!\!\text{ }}}$ and $\text{B}{{\text{B}}^{\text{ }\!\!'\!\!\text{ }}}$ are called the major and minor axes of the ellipse

\(\begin{align} & 0<\text{e}<1 \\ & \text{or } 0<{{\text{e}}^{2}}<1\quad \left( \therefore 0>-{{\text{e}}^{2}}>-1 \right) \\ & \text{or } 0<1-{{\text{e}}^{2}}<1\quad \left(\text{or } 1 \right. \left. >1-{{\text{e}}^{2}}>1-1 \right) \\ & \text{or } {{\text{a}}^{2}}\left( 1-{{\text{e}}^{2}} \right)<{{\text{a}}^{2}}\quad \left( \right.\text{or } \left. 0<1-{{\text{e}}^{2}}<1 \right) \\ & \text{or } {{b}^{2}}<{{a}^{2}} \\ & \text{i.e.} \text{b}<\text{a} \\ \end{align}\)

Double ordinates : If $P$ be a point on the ellipse draw $PN$ perpendicular to the axis of the ellipse and produced to meet the curve again at $\text{{P}'}$. Then $\text{P}{{\text{P}}^{\text{ }\!\!'\!\!\text{ }}}$ is called a double ordinate. If abscissa of $\text{P}$ is $\text{h}$ then ordinate of $\text{P}$,

$\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{~{{b}^{2}}}=1-\frac{{{h}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}$ 

$\text{y}=\frac{\text{b}}{\text{a}}\sqrt{\left( {{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{h}}^{2}} \right)}$ ( for first quadrant)

and ordinate of $\text{{P}'}$ is $y=-\frac{b}{a}\sqrt{\left( {{a}^{2}}-{{h}^{2}} \right)}$ (for fourth quadrant)

$Hence,co-ordinates\,\,of\,P\,\,and\,\,{{P}^{\prime }}\,\,are\,\,\,\,\left( h,\,\,\frac{b}{a}\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{h}}^{2}}} \right)\,\,\,and\,\,\,\left( \text{h},\,\,-\frac{\text{b}}{\text{a}}\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{h}}^{2}}} \right)\,\,\,respectively.$

Latus rectum : The double ordinates $L{{\text{L}}^{\text{ }\!\!'\!\!\text{ }}}$ and ${{L}_{1}}\text{L}_{1}^{\text{ }\!\!'\!\!\text{ }}$ are latus-rectums of the ellipse. These line are perpendicular to major axis $\text{{A}'}\,\text{A}$ and through the foci $\text{S}$ and ${S}'$ respectively.

Length of the latus rectum 

Now let ${{L}^{\prime }}{{L}^{\prime }}=2k$

then $\text{LS}={{\text{L}}^{\prime }}\text{S}=\text{k}$

$Co-ordinates\,\,of\,\text{L}\,and\,{{\text{L}}^{\prime }}\,are\,\,(\text{ae},\text{k})\,\,and\,\,(\text{ae},-\text{k})\,\,lies\,\,on\,\,the\,\,ellipse$

\(\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1\)

∴ $\frac{{{a}^{2}}{{e}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{k}^{2}}}{~{{b}^{2}}}=1$

or ${{k}^{2}}={{\text{b}}^{2}}\left( 1-{{\text{e}}^{2}} \right)$

$={{b}^{2}}\left( \frac{{{b}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}} \right)$ \(\left[ \,.{{\text{b}}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}\,\,\,(1-{{\text{e}}^{2}}) \right]\)

∴ $\text{k}=\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{a}}$ $(.\,\,\text{k}\,\,>\,\,0)$

∴ $2\text{k}=\frac{2~{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{a}}=\text{L}{{\text{L}}^{\prime }}$

∴ Length of latus rectum $L{{L}^{\prime }}={{L}_{1}}\,L_{1}^{\prime }=\frac{2{{b}^{2}}}{a}$ and end of points of latus-rectum are

$\text{L}\equiv \left( \text{ae},\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{a}} \right);{{\text{L}}^{\prime }}\equiv \left( \text{ae},-\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{a}} \right)$

${{\text{L}}_{1}}\equiv \left( -\text{ae},\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{a}} \right);\text{L}_{1}^{\prime }\equiv \left( -\text{ae},-\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{a}} \right)$

respectively.

  1. Focal chord : A chord of the ellipse passing through its focus is called a focal chord.
  2. Vertices : The vertices of the ellipse are the points where the ellipse meets its major axis.

Hence $\text{A}$ and $\text{{A}'}$ are the vertices

∴ $\text{A}\,\,\,\equiv \,\,(\text{a},\,\,0)\,\,\,and\,\,\,\,{{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\,\,\equiv \,\,(-\text{a},\,\,0)$

Eccentricity of the Ellipse $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1,\,\,a>b$ 

For the ellipse $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$

${{b}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}\left( 1-{{e}^{2}} \right)\,\,\,\Rightarrow \,\,{{e}^{2}}=1-\frac{{{b}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}=1-\frac{4{{b}^{2}}}{4{{a}^{2}}}=1-{{\left( \frac{2b}{2a} \right)}^{2}}$

$\Rightarrow \text{e}=\sqrt{1-{{\left( \frac{\text{ }Minor axis\text{ }}{\text{ }Major axis } \right)}^{2}}}$

This formula gives the eccentricity of the ellipse.

concepts.title: Comparison between both the forms of ellipse and the case when vertex not at (0,0)
concepts.conceptBody:
Equation of ellipse in other forms

In the equation of the ellipse \(\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1\), if \(a>b\) or \(a^2>b^2\) (denominator of \(x^2\) is greater than that of \(\mathrm{y}^2\) ), then the major and minor axes lie along \(\mathrm{x}\)-axis and \(\mathrm{y}\)-axis respectively But if \(\mathrm{a}<\mathrm{b}\) or \(\mathrm{a}^2<\mathrm{b}^2\) (denominator of \(\mathrm{x}^2\) is less than that of \(\mathrm{y}^2\) ), then the major axis of the ellipse lies along the \(\mathrm{y}\)-axis and is of length \(2 \mathrm{~b}\) and the minor axis along the \(\mathrm{x}\)-axis and is of length \(2 \mathrm{a}\). The coordinates of foci \(\mathrm{S}\) and \(S^{\prime}\) are \((0\), be) and \(\left(0,-\right.\) be) respectively. The equations of the directrices \(Z K\) and \(Z^{\prime} K^{\prime}\) are \(y= \pm b / e\) and eccentricity e is given by the formula

${{a}^{2}}={{b}^{2}}\left( 1-{{e}^{2}} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }e=\sqrt{1-\frac{{{a}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}}$

Difference Between Both Ellipses 

\(\begin{array}{|c|c|c|} \hline \text { Basic fund amentals } & \frac{\mathrm{x}^2}{\mathrm{a}^2}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{\mathrm{~b}^2}=\mathbf{1}, \mathrm{a}>\mathrm{b} & \frac{\mathrm{x}^2}{\mathrm{a}^2}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{\mathrm{~b}^2}=\mathbf{1}, \mathrm{b}>\mathrm{a} \\\hline \text { Centre } & (0,0) & (0,0) \\\text { Vertices } & ( \pm \mathrm{a}, 0) & (0, \pm \mathrm{b}) \\\text { Length of Major axis } & 2 \mathrm{a} & 2 \mathrm{~b} \\\text { Length of Minor axis } & 2 \mathrm{~b} & 2 \mathrm{a} \\\text { Foci } & ( \pm \mathrm{ae}, 0) & (0, \pm \mathrm{be}) \\\text { Equation of directrices } & \mathrm{x}= \pm \mathrm{a} / \mathrm{e} & \mathrm{y}= \pm \mathrm{b} / \mathrm{e} \\\text { Relation in a, b and e } & \mathrm{b}^2=\mathrm{a}^2\left(1-\mathrm{e}^2\right) & \mathrm{a}^2=\mathrm{b}^2\left(1-\mathrm{e}^2\right) \\\text { Length of latus rectum } & \frac{2 \mathrm{~b}^2}{\mathrm{a}} & \frac{2 \mathrm{a}^2}{\mathrm{~b}} \\\text { Ends of latus rectums } & \left( \pm \mathrm{ae}, \pm \frac{\mathrm{b}^2}{\mathrm{a}}\right) & \left( \pm \frac{\mathrm{a}^2}{\mathrm{~b}}, \pm \mathrm{be}\right) \\\text { Parametric co }- \text { ordinates } & (\mathrm{a} \cos \phi, \mathrm{b} \sin \phi) & (\mathrm{a} \cos \phi, \mathrm{b} \sin \phi) \\& & (0 \leq \phi<2 \pi) \\\text { Focal radii } & \mathrm{SP}=\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{ex}_1 \text { and } & \mathrm{SP}=\mathrm{b}-\mathrm{ey} \mathrm{y}_1 \text { and } \\\text { Sum of focal radii SP }+\mathrm{SP}= & \mathrm{S}^{\prime} \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{ex}_1 & \mathrm{~S}^{\prime} \mathrm{P}=\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{ey} \mathrm{y}_1 \\\text { Distance between foci } & 2 \mathrm{a} & 2 \mathrm{~b} \\\text { Distance between directrices } & 2 \mathrm{a} / \mathrm{e} & 2 \mathrm{be} \\\hline \end{array}\) 

SPECIAL FORM : If the centre of the ellipse is at point (h, k) and the directions of the axes are parallel to the coordinate axes, then its equation is

$\frac{{{(\text{x}-\text{h})}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{(\text{y}-\text{k})}^{2}}}{~{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1$

If we shift the origin at (h, k) without rotating the coordinate axes, then

$\text{x}=\text{X}+\text{h}\,and\,\text{y}=\text{Y}+\text{k}$

So, the equation of the ellipse with respect to new origin, becomes

$\frac{{{X}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{Y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$
